lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ah... magandang araw, anak. Halika, upo ka muna dito sa lilim.
lines.english: Ah... good day, child. Come, sit here in the shade first.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ako si Padre Miguel. Matagal na akong nandito sa Baryo Alon — mas matagal pa sa mga puno ng niyog na 'yan.
lines.english: I'm Padre Miguel. I've been here in Baryo Alon for a long time — longer than those coconut trees there.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Dito sa plasa, madalas akong nakaupo. Nagmamasid. Nag-iisip. Ang buhay, anak... parang alon sa dagat. Dumadating, umaalis. Paulit-ulit.
lines.english: Here at the plaza, I often sit. Observing. Thinking. Life, child... is like waves in the sea. They come, they go. Over and over.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Kung gusto mo matuto — hindi lang ng salita, kundi ng puso ng mga Pilipino — bumalik ka dito sa akin.
lines.english: If you want to learn — not just words, but the heart of the Filipino people — come back to me here.

choices.tagalog: Salamat po, Padre. Babalik po ako.
choices.english: Thank you, Padre. I'll come back.

choices.tagalog: Ano po ang matututunan ko sa inyo?
choices.english: What can I learn from you?

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Anak, alam mo ba kung ano ang 'kilig'?
lines.english: Child, do you know what 'kilig' is?

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Walang katumbas ang kilig sa English. Hindi siya 'happy' lang. Hindi rin 'excited' lang.
lines.english: Kilig has no equivalent in English. It's not just 'happy.' Not just 'excited' either.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Kilig... 'yun ang nararamdaman mo pag may hawak ang kamay mo na hindi mo inaasahan. 'Yung biglaang tibok ng puso. 'Yung ngiti na hindi mo mapigilan.
lines.english: Kilig... it's what you feel when someone unexpectedly holds your hand. That sudden heartbeat. That smile you can't stop.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ang mga Pilipino, marunong maramdaman ang kilig sa maliliit na bagay. Isang tingin. Isang ngiti. 'Yun lang.
lines.english: Filipinos know how to feel kilig in the smallest things. One glance. One smile. That's all it takes.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Hindi kailangan ng malaking drama para sa kilig. Minsan, tahimik lang — pero ramdam na ramdam mo sa dibdib.
lines.english: You don't need big drama for kilig. Sometimes, it's just quiet — but you feel it deeply in your chest.

choices.tagalog: Ang ganda naman po noon, Padre.
Parang nararamdaman ko na po ang kilig!
choices.english: I think I can feel kilig now!

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Anak, may gusto akong ituro sa iyo ngayon. Tungkol sa 'utang na loob.'
lines.english: Child, there's something I want to teach you today. About 'utang na loob.'

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Literal na 'utang' — ibig sabihin ay debt. 'Loob' — inside, ang kalooban mo. So 'utang na loob' — isang utang na nasa loob mo. Sa puso.
lines.english: Literally 'utang' — meaning debt. 'Loob' — inside, your inner self. So 'utang na loob' — a debt that's inside you. In the heart.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Kapag may tumulong sa iyo — totohanang tulong, 'yung tipong binago ang buhay mo — hindi mo 'yun malilimutan. Dala-dala mo 'yun habang buhay.
lines.english: When someone helps you — real help, the kind that changed your life — you can never forget it. You carry it with you for life.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Hindi 'yan negatibo, ha? Hindi 'yan parang utang sa bangko. Ito ay... utang ng puso. Isang pagkilala na may nagmahal sa iyo nang walang hinihintay na kapalit.
lines.english: It's not negative, okay? It's not like a bank debt. This is... a debt of the heart. An acknowledgment that someone loved you without expecting anything in return.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Isipin mo si Aling Nena — pinalain ka niya. Binigyan ng pagkain, ng payo. Hindi niya hinihingi na bayaran mo siya. Pero sa loob mo... alam mong may utang ka. 'Yan ang utang na loob.
lines.english: Think of Aling Nena — she took care of you. Gave you food, advice. She doesn't ask you to pay her. But inside... you know you owe something. That's utang na loob.

choices.tagalog: Naiintindihan ko na po, Padre. Ang lalim.
choices.english: I understand now, Padre. It's so deep.

choices.tagalog: Ganoon pala 'yun... salamat po sa pagtuturo.
choices.english: So that's what it is... thank you for teaching me.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: May isang expression ang mga Pilipino na ginagamit halos araw-araw: 'Bahala na.'
lines.english: There's one expression Filipinos use almost every day: 'Bahala na.'

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Galing siya sa 'Bathala' — ang lumang salita para sa Diyos. 'Bahala na' — 'Bahala na si Bathala.' Let God take care of it.
lines.english: It comes from 'Bathala' — the old word for God. 'Bahala na' — 'Let Bathala handle it.' Let God take care of it.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ang iba nagsasabing tamad 'yan. 'Bahala na' — parang sumuko ka. Pero hindi ko 'yan pinaniniwalaan.
lines.english: Some say that's laziness. 'Bahala na' — as if you're giving up. But I don't believe that.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Para sa akin, ang 'bahala na' ay tapang. Ginawa mo na ang lahat ng makakaya mo — tapos ipinaubaya mo na sa taas. Hindi surrender 'yan. Tiwala 'yan.
lines.english: For me, 'bahala na' is courage. You've done everything you can — then you leave it to the heavens. That's not surrender. That's trust.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Kaya pag naririnig mo ang isang Pilipino na nagsabing 'Bahala na!' bago tumalon sa dagat — hindi siya sumusuko. Lumululon siya sa buhay. Buong tapang.
lines.english: So when you hear a Filipino say 'Bahala na!' before jumping into the sea — they're not giving up. They're diving into life. With full courage.

choices.tagalog: Bahala na! Haha. Salamat po, Padre.
choices.english: Bahala na! Haha. Thank you, Padre.

choices.tagalog: Gusto ko po 'yun — tapang at tiwala.
choices.english: I like that — courage and trust.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Nandito ka ulit... maganda 'yan. Ang matutong makinig ay mas mahirap kaysa matutong magsalita.
lines.english: You're here again... that's good. Learning to listen is harder than learning to speak.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Alam mo, anak, ang wika ay hindi lang salita. Ang wika ay buhay. Kapag natutunan mo ang salita ng isang tao, natutunan mo ang mundo niya.
lines.english: You know, child, language isn't just words. Language is life. When you learn someone's words, you learn their world.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Kaya huwag kang magmadali. Dahan-dahan lang. Ang Tagalog, parang dagat — malalim, pero puno ng buhay.
lines.english: So don't rush. Take it slow. Tagalog, like the sea — deep, but full of life.

choices.english: Thank you for the wisdom, Padre.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Magandang umaga. Tahimik pa ang plasa... pinakagusto ko 'tong oras. Bago pa gumising ang lahat.
lines.english: Good morning. The plaza is still quiet... this is my favorite time. Before everyone wakes up.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Pag umaga, naririnig mo ang dagat mula dito. Ang alon, parang nagdadasal rin.
lines.english: In the morning, you can hear the sea from here. The waves, as if they're praying too.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ang bawat umaga ay bagong simula. Huwag mong kalimutan 'yan, anak.
lines.english: Every morning is a new beginning. Don't forget that, child.

choices.tagalog: Ang tahimik nga po dito, Padre.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Gabi na... nakikita mo ba ang buwan? Malaki ngayong gabi.
lines.english: It's night... do you see the moon? It's big tonight.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Noong bata pa ako, sinasabi ng lolo ko na pag malaki ang buwan, maganda ang huli bukas. Maswerte raw.
lines.english: When I was young, my grandfather said that when the moon is big, the catch tomorrow is good. Lucky, they said.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Matulog ka na, anak. Pahinga. Ang mga sagot sa tanong mo — darating din 'yan. Sa tamang oras.
lines.english: Go to sleep now, child. Rest. The answers to your questions — they'll come. In the right time.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ah... kape. Galing kay Kapitan? Salamat, anak. Kailangan ko nga nito.
lines.english: Ah... coffee. From the Captain? Thank you, child. I do need this.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Ang kape, alam mo, parang pag-uusap — mainit sa simula, tapos dahan-dahang lumalamig. Pero masarap hanggang sa huli.
lines.english: Coffee, you know, is like a conversation — hot at the start, then slowly cools down. But good until the end.

lines.speaker: padre_miguel
lines.tagalog: Nako, tignan mo ako, nag-pipilosopo na naman. Haha. Salamat, anak. Mabait ka.
lines.english: Oh, look at me, philosophizing again. Haha. Thank you, child. You're kind.

choices.tagalog: Walang anuman po, Padre. Masaya akong tumulong.
choices.english: You're welcome, Padre. I'm happy to help.
